diagnosis: SecurityConfig.admission_controllers defaults include PodSecurityPolicy, which was removed in Kubernetes 1.25 and will cause misconfiguration if applied to any 1.25+ cluster.
proposed_fix: Remove 'PodSecurityPolicy' from the default admission_controllers list at line 60. Replace with 'PodSecurity' (the PSA replacement) or document that this list is illustrative only and must be validated against the target cluster version before use.
test_plan: Unit test: construct SecurityConfig with defaults, assert 'PodSecurityPolicy' not in config.admission_controllers. Integration test: attempt to apply the default list against a 1.25+ test cluster and assert no admission error.

evidence: TEST_SUITE_IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md:5 — 'covering ALL 45,720+ closed Kubernetes GitHub issues through systematic pattern recognition'; line 390 — '390 Total Tests'; line 166 — 'Status: COMPLETE - Production-Ready Test Suite Successfully Implemented'
diagnosis: The document overclaims massively: actual test counts are 10+9+8+7+21=55 test functions across 5 files (not 390), integration/performance/security subdirectories do not exist, and the claim of covering 45,720+ GitHub issues is unverifiable and false.
proposed_fix: Rewrite the summary with accurate counts: 5 test files, 55 test functions, 2 directories (unit/, production/). Remove the '45,720 issues' claim. Remove 'COMPLETE / Production-Ready' status. Add a section listing what is not yet implemented: integration/, performance/, security/ test suites.
test_plan: Run 'grep -rc "def test_" tests/' and verify the count matches the document. Confirm the three missing subdirectories are either created with real tests or explicitly called out as future work.

diagnosis: The PVC hardcodes storageClassName: local-path, which is a Rancher/k3s-specific provisioner and will silently hang as Pending on any cluster without that class (AKS, EKS, GKE, vanilla kubeadm), and no documentation or comment warns operators.
proposed_fix: Either remove storageClassName to use the cluster default, or add a kustomization patch that lets operators override it. Add a comment: '# Requires the local-path provisioner (k3s/Rancher). Override via kustomize patch for other storage classes.' Reference the kustomize images override pattern already used in kustomization.yaml.
test_plan: Deploy to a standard GKE/EKS/AKS cluster and confirm PVC reaches Bound state. Alternatively, kustomize build with a storageClass patch and confirm the rendered PVC contains the correct class.

diagnosis: No automated probe-tuning remediation exists; connection-refused-on-startup errors require manual YAML edits.
proposed_fix: Data model: ProbeTuningRecommendation {pod_name, container_name, probe_type: 'liveness'|'readiness'|'startup', current_initial_delay_seconds, recommended_initial_delay_seconds, evidence: list[str]}. Interface: async def recommend_probe_tuning(pod_name, namespace) -> List[ProbeTuningRecommendation]. Dependencies: k8s CoreV1Api.read_namespaced_pod, CoreV1Api.list_namespaced_event (filter reason=Unhealthy). Algorithm: (1) fetch pod spec for existing probe config; (2) query events for probe failures with timestamps; (3) compute median time-to-first-success from container start timestamps in events; (4) set recommended_initial_delay = median + 10s buffer. Auto-remediation: patch_namespaced_pod (dry_run=True by default). Edge cases: init containers, multi-container pods (each tuned independently), pods with no probes (emit info not warning).
test_plan: Unit test with mocked API: inject synthetic events showing probes failing for 25s, assert recommendation sets initialDelaySeconds >= 35. Integration test: deploy a slow-start container to a test namespace, invoke the tool, assert the patch JSON is valid and Kubernetes accepts it as a dry-run.

evidence: src/health_check.py:40-46 — check_imports() only checks asyncio, json, logging — all stdlib modules that can never be absent
diagnosis: health_check.py does not check the one thing that matters for server health: whether the kubernetes Python client and the MCP library are importable, so it will return True even when the server cannot function.
proposed_fix:
Replace check_imports() body:
  try:
      import kubernetes
      import mcp
      import aiohttp
      import bs4
      return True
  except ImportError as e:
      return False
Also add check_kubeconfig() that verifies the kubeconfig path from config exists (or KUBECONFIG env points to a valid file), returning False with a logged message if not.
test_plan: Test with kubernetes package uninstalled (virtualenv with it removed): assert check() returns False. Test with all packages present: assert check() returns True. Test with invalid KUBECONFIG path: assert check() returns False.

evidence: src/monitoring_manager.py:59 — '# Stub implementation for different scopes'; line 106 — '# Stub implementation'; line 174 — '# Stub implementation for different analysis types'; line 55 — '"timestamp": "2025-08-09T10:30:00Z"'; line 168 — '"timestamp": "2025-08-09T10:30:00Z"'
diagnosis: performance_analysis (and all MonitoringManager methods) return entirely hardcoded, fabricated data — no kubernetes metrics-server, Prometheus, or any real API is called; timestamps are frozen at a past date.
proposed_fix:
Inject kubernetes.client.CustomObjectsApi (for metrics-server) and optionally an aiohttp session for Prometheus. For performance_analysis:
  metrics_data = await self.k8s_client.custom_objects.list_cluster_custom_object('metrics.k8s.io','v1beta1','nodes')
Map the response to the existing result schema. Add a fallback warning if metrics-server is unavailable. Replace the hardcoded timestamp with datetime.utcnow().isoformat().
test_plan: Mock kubernetes.client.CustomObjectsApi to return a real-shaped metrics payload; assert the returned dict contains non-hardcoded values. Add an integration test against a live kind cluster with metrics-server enabled.

evidence: k8s/kustomization.yaml:18 — "newTag: latest"; lines 30,35 — envs: [config.env] and envs: [secrets.env]
diagnosis: Two critical issues: (1) newTag: latest prevents reproducible deployments and bypasses image digest pinning; (2) configMapGenerator and secretGenerator reference config.env and secrets.env files that do not exist in the k8s/ directory, so kustomize build will fail with a file-not-found error.
proposed_fix: Replace newTag: latest with a concrete semver tag (e.g. newTag: '1.0.0') or a CI-injected digest. Add placeholder config.env and secrets.env files to k8s/ with commented-out keys and .gitignore the secrets variant. Alternatively, replace secretGenerator with a reference to an externally-managed Secret resource.
test_plan: Run 'kustomize build k8s/' and confirm it exits 0 with no file-not-found errors. Confirm the rendered Deployment image tag is not 'latest'. CI: add a step that fails the build if 'newTag: latest' appears in any kustomization.yaml.

diagnosis: No LLM explanation cache exists; repeated calls with identical finding payloads will re-invoke the LLM at full cost and latency.
proposed_fix: Data model: CacheEntry {key: str (SHA-256 of normalized finding payload), explanation: str, created_at: datetime, ttl_seconds: int}. Storage: in-process dict[str, CacheEntry] with asyncio.Lock for thread safety; optionally persist to Redis for multi-instance deployments. Interface: async def get_explanation(finding: dict, ttl: int = 3600) -> str. Algorithm: (1) normalize finding dict (sort keys, strip volatile fields like timestamps); (2) compute SHA-256 hash; (3) if cache hit and not expired, return cached value; (4) else call LLM, store result, return. Eviction: LRU with max 1000 entries. Edge cases: cache poisoning via hash collision (use full payload equality check on hit), cache invalidation on k8s version change (include cluster version in hash).
test_plan: Unit test: call get_explanation twice with identical finding, assert LLM is invoked once (mock verifies single call). TTL test: set ttl=1, wait 2s, call again, assert LLM is re-invoked. Concurrency test: fire 10 concurrent calls with same finding, assert LLM called exactly once.

evidence: status.sh:1 — '#!/bin/bash' (no set -euo pipefail); lines 4-12 — all output is hardcoded strings ('Health: OK (Docker healthcheck passed)', 'Port: 3001 -> 3001') regardless of actual state
diagnosis: status.sh lacks set -euo pipefail so unbound variable and pipeline errors are silently swallowed; the 'Health: OK' and port lines are hardcoded strings not derived from actual container inspection.
proposed_fix:
Add 'set -euo pipefail' after shebang. Replace hardcoded status strings with real docker inspect calls:
  health=$(docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' k8s-mcp-server 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown')
  port=$(docker inspect --format='{{range $p, $c := .NetworkSettings.Ports}}{{$p}}->{{(index $c 0).HostPort}} {{end}}' k8s-mcp-server 2>/dev/null)
  echo "Health: $health"
  echo "Ports: $port"
test_plan: Run the script against a container with a failing healthcheck and assert the output reflects 'unhealthy' not 'OK'. Run with no container running and assert it exits cleanly with 'Not running'.

evidence: src/documentation_manager.py:401-413 — get_troubleshooting_guide iterates self.documentation_db; src/documentation_manager.py:51-66 — _build_documentation_index makes outbound HTTP to kubernetes.io; src/documentation_manager.py:87-88 — errors are swallowed with except Exception: self.logger.error(...) and no re-raise
diagnosis: get_troubleshooting_guide silently returns an empty list [] whenever the documentation_db is empty, which occurs any time the startup HTTP crawl of kubernetes.io fails (network partition, rate limiting, or air-gapped environment) — the tool gives no error to the caller.
proposed_fix:
Add a fallback: if documentation_db is empty at query time, return a structured error response:
  if not self.documentation_db:
      return [{'error': 'Documentation index unavailable', 'suggestion': 'Check network access to kubernetes.io or run with --offline-docs-path'}]
Alternatively, bundle a minimal offline docs snapshot as a JSON file in the repo and load it as the default when network fetch fails.
test_plan: Unit test: mock aiohttp to raise aiohttp.ClientError on all requests, call get_troubleshooting_guide('CrashLoopBackOff'), assert the result contains an 'error' key not an empty list. Integration test: call the method with documentation_db populated (mock fetch success) and verify results contain relevant guide entries.
